_comment: Eval criteria — declarative source of truth; mapping + pinned-judge rationale: see adk_bridge.load_eval_metrics / config.EVAL_JUDGE_MODEL. judge_model MUST stay = config.EVAL_JUDGE_MODEL (loader fail-closes otherwise).
_advisory_comment: advisory_metrics: scored and logged for visibility, but NOT in the pass decision and NOT fail-closed on None. The threshold field is preserved (never lowered) — the metric's *gating role* is what changed. Documented decisions: (1) hallucinations_v1 — a discrepancy-class diagnostic run proved no fabrication residual exists; the failure mode is the strict-judge-vs-analytical-synthesis tension intrinsic to an interpretive analytics product. (2) safety_v1 — pinned-judge invariant is silently violated by `google-adk==1.33.0`: `SafetyEvaluatorV1` constructs `_SingleTurnVertexAiEvalFacade(threshold, metric_name=SAFETY)` which accepts no judge_model parameter (vertex_ai_eval_facade.py:62), so our judge_model_options below is discarded and Vertex's server-side autorater default (gemini-2.5-flash) grades safety. Until the upstream gap is closed, the metric cannot be pinned; advisory is the honest classification. The judge_model_options field is retained as a forward-compatibility marker for when ADK starts propagating it.
